_doc.schema: Each object in 'tasks' is one study task. Fields:
_doc.id: Unique string identifier (e.g. 'T5'). Used in log events.
_doc.encodings: Optional. Array of encoding names to activate for this task. If omitted, the default set for the condition is used. Categorical options: 'color', 'dashing'. Numerical options: 'color', 'thickness'. Directional options: 'gradient', 'taper', 'arrows'. Examples: ['color'] for colour-only, ['dashing'] for dash-only, ['color','dashing'] for both.
_doc.filter: Optional. Object that filters the dataset before rendering. Omit for the full dataset. Supported types: { 'type': 'deduplicate' } — keeps exactly one edge per node pair (the first one in insertion order), turning a dense bidirectional multigraph into a clean directed graph. Use this for directional tasks so each pair has an unambiguous direction.
_doc.description: Question text shown to the participant.
_doc.colorKeywords: Optional. Array of { word, color } objects. Each occurrence of 'word' in description is wrapped in a coloured <span>.
_doc.options: Required for multiple-choice only. Array of option strings.
_doc.correctAnswers: Array of accepted correct answers. All strings must match selectedAnswer exactly. Use a single-element array for one correct answer, or multiple elements when several answers should count as correct (e.g. both endpoints of an edge).
_doc.diagram: Path to the dataset JSON (currently always 'data/sampled_data.json').
_doc.howToAdd: Append a new object to the 'tasks' array. Tasks run in array order. The progress bar adjusts automatically. If the new task uses a different condition than the previous one, the canvas rebuilds automatically. If the new task changes only the 'encodings' but keeps the same condition, the canvas also rebuilds with the new encodings.

tasks.description: Which country of origin has the most airline routes in this network?
Find the busiest airport hub, the one where routes from the most countries converge. Look for the node where the greatest variety of edge colors and dash patterns meets.
Find the airport connected by the longest route in this network. Look for the thickest and most intensely colored edge, then identify the airport at one of its ends.
Which airport is the departure point for the most outgoing flights? Edges taper from wide at the source to narrow at the destination, and shift from green (source) to blue (target).
